page: Amazon Web Services r7a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, which provides a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include any local NVMe storage or onboard GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage options.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.4xlarge demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core metrics, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with uncached read speeds at 75.99 GB/sec and write speeds at 54.91 GB/sec. Notably, the instance achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small-scale language models, reaching 344.43 tokens/sec.

Given its high-density DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, the r7a.4xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local storage or GPU acceleration. The instance is suited for memory-intensive applications such as database operations, caching layers, text processing, and small-scale language model inference. A baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps supports moderate data transfer requirements.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, it is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or a GPU, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the r7a.4xlarge exhibits strong single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. Its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and text processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 6 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
